page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal distinct characteristics and architectural tradeoffs. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% according to stress-ng benchmarks, indicating limited suitability for single-threaded, high-frequency execution. However, multi-core CPU performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory performance follows a similar pattern: cached small-block read bandwidth is average, whereas uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak, falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6a.8xlarge offers a standard resource density suitable for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe drives or specialized accelerators. It is best suited for general-purpose applications such as static web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, Redis database operations, and moderate database workloads where balanced CPU-to-memory ratios are required.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor. It delivers 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps and contains no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and uncached large-block memory bandwidth are weak, while multi-core processing, cached memory operations, and database tasks perform at average levels. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that do not demand high single-thread speeds. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data decompression.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced multi-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose KVM instance featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and average multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and system memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 vCPUs and physical cores
128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core CPU and cached memory performance
Weak single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and multi-threaded workloads
